content.text:
Emerging Technologies and Information Systems
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF32  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamental concepts of emerging technologies (AI, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, IoT, Big Data, etc.)
Analyse the impact of these technologies on organisational information systems
Acquire a critical vision of the opportunities and challenges related to these technologies
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems, networks.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Emerging Technologies
Definition and classification of emerging technologies
Overview of main technologies (AI, Blockchain, Cloud computing, IoT, Big data, Cybersecurity, Social networks)
Artificial Intelligence and Information Systems
Machine Learning
Natural Language Processing (NLP)
Computer vision
Impact on decision-making, personalisation and automation
Blockchain and Information Systems
Fundamental principles of Blockchain
Applications in supply chain management, finance, etc.
Smart contracts
Risks and challenges of Blockchain
Cloud Computing and Information Systems
Deployment models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)
Cloud services (storage, compute, databases)
Cloud security; impact on agility and scalability
Internet of Things (IoT) and Information Systems
IoT architecture and technologies
IoT data collection and processing
Applications in home automation, health, industry
IoT data security and privacy
Big Data and Information Systems
Big Data concepts and challenges
Big Data processing tools and technologies
Data analysis and business intelligence
Information Systems and Cybersecurity
IS threats and vulnerabilities
Protection and incident response strategies
Risk management
Information Systems and Social Networks
Social networks as sources of information and disinformation
Impacts of social networks on enterprise IS
Security and privacy challenges
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous assessment, mini-project
Mini-project on the different technologies proposed at the beginning of each chapter, evaluated as a report and oral presentation.
References
Stuart Russell and Peter Norvig (2021).
Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
. Fourth edition. Pearson.
Daniel Drescher (2017).
Blockchain Basics
. Apress.
Thomas Erl, Zaigham Mahmood, Ricardo Puttini (2013).
Cloud Computing: Concepts, Technology & Architecture
. Prentice-Hall.
Aurélien Géron (2019).
Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras & TensorFlow
.
Daniel Jurafsky and James H. Martin (2025).
Speech and Language Processing
. 3rd edition.
Vermesan, O., & Friess, P. (2014).
Internet of things applications
. Taylor & Francis.
P.W. Singer and Allan Friedman (2014).
Cybersecurity and Cyberwar: What Everyone Needs to Know
. Oxford University Press.
